title: Simon Property Group: This Price Is Just Plain Silly
text: Simon Property Group is a high-quality REIT that's well-positioned to weather current storms. It has a strong focus on high-end and outlet retail, two of the more e-commerce resistant retail subsectors.

title: 7 Highest-Yielding S&P 500 Stocks to Grab Now as the Bear Market Rally Wilts

title: The 7 Best Real Estate Stocks to Buy Now
text: Wall Street isn't shielded from a recession, and with inflation running rampant, it's tough for investors to find places to hide. However, traders have started paying more attention than ever before to real estate stocks, as they see them becoming less volatile compared to other markets during tough financial times.

title: Realty Income And Simon Property Q2: Recession Readiness Assessment
text: Realty Income's long lease durations provide stability during uncertain times, but the downside is that rent raises are slow. Simon Property Group, in contrast, is more cyclical given the discretionary nature of its mall properties.

title: 3 Cheap REITs That Are Too Good to Ignore
text: The largest U.S.-listed exchange-traded fund (ETF) that's focused on real estate investment trusts, or REITs, is the Vanguard Real Estate ETF (NYSEARCA: VNQ ), with $38.2 billion in total net assets. Down 14% year-t0-date through Aug. 8, there are plenty of cheap REITs to buy among its 170 real estate stocks.

title: Simon Property Q2 Results Show Consumers, Retailers Still Love Going to the Mall
text: With a portfolio of more than 230 properties spread across 37 states and three continents, Indianapolis-based Simon Property Group's purview of — and exposure to — the global retail industry is second to none.

title: Mall owners say retailers are still opening stores in spite of recession fears
text: Retailers in the U.S. have announced 4,432 store openings so far this year, compared with 1,954 store closings, according to data from Coresight Research.

title: My Top 5 Dividend Stocks For The 2nd Half Of 2022 (My First 5 Picks Beat The S&P By 10%)
text: The five stocks I picked at the beginning of the year collectively outperformed the S&P 500 by roughly 10%, excluding dividends. On average, my five picks for the latter half of the year have bigger yields and higher 5-year dividend growth rates.

title: Simon Property (SPG) Q2 FFO Beat Estimates
text: Simon Property (SPG) delivered FFO and revenue surprises of 1.72% and 1.33%, respectively, for the quarter ended June 2022. Do the numbers hold clues to what lies ahead for the stock?
